title: 🎙️ Science, Wonder & the Natural World
description: Today we look at scientific discovery and the natural world — from ecology and wildlife research to astronomy and deep-sea biology. We're grounding these stories in the landscapes and living systems of the Cariboo: what salmon runs, boreal forest research, or species-at-risk studies tell us about the wild places we live beside. We're after stories that inspire curiosity and wonder about how the world works. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today we look at scientific discovery and the natural world — from ecology and wildlife research to astronomy and deep-sea biology. We're grounding these stories in the landscapes and living systems of the Cariboo: what salmon runs, boreal forest research, or species-at-risk studies tell us about the wild places we live beside. We're after stories that inspire curiosity and wonder about how the world works.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Science, Wonder & the Natural World' — scientific discovery, ecology, natural phenomena, and the curiosity-driven study of living systems. Strong fits include: wildlife biology and species research, ecology studies and biodiversity findings, watershed and salmon research relevant to BC, astronomy and space science, new species discoveries, citizen science projects, natural history stories, wildfire science and forest ecology, climate science findings (not just policy), geology, evolution, and deep-time Earth stories, and any scientific research grounded in BC or Cariboo landscapes and ecosystems. Prefer articles that convey genuine curiosity and discovery — the 'how' and 'why' of the natural world. Score 70–90 for: prehistoric life and palaeontology (ancient oceans, Cretaceous ecology, deep-time species), new species discoveries in ocean or remote environments, deep-sea biology, evolutionary biology findings, natural history stories with a strong sense of wonder, and field research grounded in BC ecosystems. Penalise (score 0–30): consumer health and pharmaceutical research without ecology or natural-world context; pure policy and advocacy news; technology business stories without a science or ecology angle; human interest and viral news without scientific substance; geopolitical and crime news. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [CBC Arts] Director Carl Rinsch sentenced to 2½ years in Netflix fraud case
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

Hollywood writer-director Carl Rinsch was sentenced Monday to two-and-half-years in prison after being convicted of defrauding Netflix out of $11 million US for a never-finished sci-fi series. Supporters including Keanu Reeves had asked the court to show leniency.
items.summary: Hollywood writer-director Carl Rinsch was sentenced Monday to two-and-half-years in prison after being convicted of defrauding Netflix out of $11 million US for a never-finished sci-fi series. Supporters including Keanu Reeves had asked the court to show leniency.

items.title: [Boing Boing] Americans now lose a quarter of a trillion dollars a year gambling
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

Americans will lose about $250 billion gambling this year, reports Joey Politano, a number that's up 60% in half a decade. And that doesn't include "prediction markets" and cryptocurrencies, both mostly vehicles for more gambling. Politicians are well-greased by the industry lobbyists, personal debt is soaring out of sight, and there's no immediate prospect of anyone taking control. — Read the rest

The post Americans now lose a quarter of a trillion dollars a year gambling appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [New Atlas] FAA updates regulations for new supersonic age
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

The US Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) is clearing the way for the return of commercial supersonic air travel by revamping regulations for faster-than-sound aircraft for the first time since 1973 – to reflect progress in aviation engineering and the removal of prejudicial attitudes.

items.title: [CFJC Today Kamloops] NorthPaws win Canada Day battle, drop three-game series against Falcons
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

File photo. (Image Credit: CFJC Today) By Aaron Schulze NorthPaws NorthPaws win Canada Day battle, drop three-game series against Falcons Jul 3, 2026 | 6:50 AM Share on Facebook Share on Bluesky Share on X Copy Link KAMLOOPS — The Kamloops NorthPaws dropped their three-game home-and-home-and-home series with the Kelowna Falcons. The NorthPaws were blown out by the Falcons on the road 19-7 Thursday (July 2) to close out the three-game set. It comes after dropping the first game in Kelowna on Tuesday 11-6. Overall, the NorthPaws are 0-4 in Kelowna in the 2026 West Coast League (WCL) season.

items.title: [New Atlas] Google looks to electronic waste as cost-effective AI server solution
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

Every year, billions of phones are discarded globally, many of them with perfectly usable processors. At the same time, the tech industry is preparing to spend billions on new AI computing hardware, at high environmental costs. Google, in collaboration with researchers at the University of California, San Diego, is developing a way to bridge those two realities by building a server from recovered phone processors, tackling a waste problem while providing low-carbon computing.

Continue Reading

Category: Computing, Consumer Tech, Technology

Tags: Processing, Mobile Phones, Cell+Phone, Chips
